actionLibraryList.title: Mat Lying Toe Taps
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lie on your back with arms by your sides. Bend the hips and knees to tabletop position (90°), keeping a neutral spine and core engaged. Inhale to prepare.
2.Exhale, engage the abdominals, maintain pelvic stability, and lower one leg in a controlled manner, lightly tapping the toes on the floor without moving the torso.
3.Inhale, return the leg to tabletop position.
4.Exhale, switch to the other leg and repeat the toe tap, alternating sides. Maintain core stability and controlled rhythm throughout the movement.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770688634189.jpg","context":"1.Keep the lower back stable, pressing the sacrum into the mat.\n2.Relax the shoulders and neck, allowing them to sink downward."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to engage and lower the leg.
Inhale to return with control.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid arching or sagging the lower back.
2.Do not lower the legs too quickly or use momentum to tap the toes.
3.Keep the shoulders relaxed; avoid shrugging.

actionLibraryList.title: Mat Scapula Isolation
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lie on your back with a neutral spine and pelvis. Place your feet hip-width apart on the mat. Extend your arms straight toward the ceiling with palms facing inward. Keep the shoulder blades neutral.
2.Inhale, protract the shoulder blades, creating a stretch between them.
3.Exhale, return the shoulder blades to neutral, extending and stretching the posterior shoulder girdle.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770688675769.jpg","context":"1.Keep the lower back stable, pressing the sacrum into the mat.\n2.Keep the lower limbs firmly planted and still."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to return the shoulder blades to neutral.
Inhale to protract and stretch between the shoulder blades.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid over-retracting the shoulder blades during the movement.
2.Keep the spine stable; avoid any spinal movement.

actionLibraryList.title: Mat Arm Circles
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lie on your back with a neutral spine and pelvis. Place your feet hip-width apart on the mat. Extend your arms along your sides with palms facing down.
2.Inhale, lift both arms overhead in a controlled arc. Limit the range so that the abdominals remain engaged, the shoulder blades stay stable, and the lower ribs stay in contact with the mat.
3.Exhale, sweep the arms in an arc back down along the sides to the hips. Repeat the movement.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770688692658.jpg","context":"1.Keep the lower back stable, pressing the sacrum into the mat.\n2.Keep the shoulders and neck relaxed, with the shoulders down."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Inhale to lift and extend the arms overhead.
Exhale to sweep the arms back down in a controlled arc.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid flaring the ribs or arching the lower back; maintain core control.
2.Keep the shoulders relaxed and shoulder blades stable; avoid shrugging.

actionLibraryList.title: Mat Cat & Cow
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Begin in a quadruped position with hands under the shoulders and knees under the hips. Keep the spine neutral, pelvis stable, and core engaged. Inhale to prepare.
2.Exhale, draw the abdomen inward and upward, tilt the pelvis posteriorly, and sequentially roll the spine from the tailbone, rounding the back to the highest point into Cat Pose.
3.Inhale, return the torso to a neutral position, then sequentially extend the spine from the tailbone to the crown of the head, opening the chest forward into Cow Pose.
4.Exhale, return to neutral and roll again. Flow smoothly between flexion and extension with the breath, maintaining stable shoulder blades and core engagement throughout.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770690119784.jpg","context":"1.Keep the spine and pelvis stable; avoid collapsing the lower back.\n2.Relax the shoulders and neck, letting them drop down naturally."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale, engage the abdominals, and flex the spine segment by segment.
Inhale, lengthen and open the spine.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid collapsing the lower back or over-compressing the lumbar spine, maintaining neutral control.
2.Keep the shoulders relaxed and avoid compensating with the arms.
3.Avoid moving only the head or pelvis; ensure the spine moves segment by segment.

actionLibraryList.title: Open Elbow
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Keep the pelvis and spine neutral, legs straight and together with feet flexed. Bend the elbows and place them in the handles, upper arms at shoulder height, palms facing the torso. Stabilize the shoulder blades downward and inhale to prepare.
2.Exhale. Keep the shoulder blades stable without tensing. Open the elbows outward horizontally, expanding the chest, and feel the activation in the rear shoulders and upper back.
3.Inhale. Bring the elbows forward and together to return, maintaining upper arm height and a stable torso.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770628890165.jpg","context":"1.Maintain a neutral spine.\n2.Keep the shoulders relaxed and down.\n3.Keep the shoulders and elbows aligned at the same height."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to open outward.
Inhale to bring inward.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage the shoulders, back, and core.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid shrugging or over-tightening the shoulder blades.
2.Keep the upper arms level; do not let them sink or lift.
3.Maintain a stable torso; avoid forward or backward swaying.

actionLibraryList.title: Chest Expansion
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Inhale. Engage the back and rear shoulder muscles to pull the arms backward, slightly past the sides of the body.
2.Exhale. Maintain tension in the back and slowly return the handles to the starting position, controlling the eccentric stretch.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770628913184.jpg","context":"1.Keep the torso stable.\n2.Keep the shoulders relaxed and down.\n3.Keep the arms straight."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Inhale on effort.
Exhale to return.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep the wrists neutral; avoid bending.
2.Keep the shoulders down and relaxed.
3.Maintain a stable pelvis; avoid sagging the lower back.

actionLibraryList.title: Chest Expansion External Rotation
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Engage the core and activate the shoulders and back, pulling the handles to your sides with elbows bent at 90°, inhale to prepare.
2.Exhale. Keep the upper arms stable and rotate the forearms outward to pull the ropes apart.
3.Inhale. Maintain a stable torso and use controlled eccentric movement to slowly return the ropes to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770631527732.jpg","context":"1. Keep the torso stable.\n2. Let the shoulders stay naturally down and relaxed.\n3. Keep the elbows stable by your sides."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale on effort.
Inhale to return.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Maintain a neutral spine; avoid sagging the lower back.
2.Keep the upper arms close to the body.
3.Keep the shoulders down; avoid shrugging.

actionLibraryList.title: Chest Expansion Row Rotation
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Exhale. Bend the right elbow and rotate the thoracic spine to the right, keeping the left arm extended.
2.Inhale. Slowly extend the right arm, controlling the return.
3.Switch sides. Exhale, bend the left elbow and rotate the thoracic spine to the left, keeping the right arm extended. Inhale, slowly extend the left arm back with control.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770631344263.jpg","context":"1.Keep the torso stable.\n2.Keep the shoulders relaxed and down."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Inhale on effort.
Exhale to return.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage the shoulders, back, and arms.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep the pelvis and lower body stable; avoid sagging the lower back.
2.Keep the shoulders down and relaxed.
3.Return slowly; avoid releasing too quickly.

actionLibraryList.title: Draw a Sword
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Exhale and engage your arms and shoulders to lift your arm diagonally upward along the opposite diagonal.
2.Keep your torso facing forward and maintain a tight core throughout to prevent rotation or side-tilt.
3.Inhale and eccentrically control the handle back to the starting position, resisting the cable tension and avoiding a rapid pull.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770628025775.jpg","context":"1.Keep your shoulders down.\n2.Maintain a neutral spine.\n3.Keep your pelvis stable."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to lift your arm diagonally upward.
Inhale to slowly return the handle.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep your shoulders down and neck relaxed.
2.Keep your torso stable, avoiding rotation or sagging.

actionLibraryList.title: Side-Kneeling Arm Press
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Start with one arm bent at your side. Inhale to press the arm upward until fully extended.
2.Exhale and bend the arm to slowly lower it back to the starting position.
3.Keep your torso facing forward throughout the movement.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770694148745.jpg","context":"1.Keep your shoulders down.\n2.Maintain a neutral spine.\n3.Keep your pelvis stable."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to exert force.
Inhale to slowly return.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep your shoulders down; avoid shrugging.
2.Maintain your center of gravity without swaying.

actionLibraryList.title: Draw a Sword
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Exhale and engage your arms and shoulders to lift your arm diagonally upward along the opposite diagonal.
2.Keep your torso facing forward and maintain a tight core throughout to prevent rotation or side-tilt.
3.Inhale and eccentrically control the handle back to the starting position, resisting the cable tension and avoiding a rapid pull.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770628025775.jpg","context":"1.Keep your shoulders down.\n2.Maintain a neutral spine.\n3.Keep your pelvis stable."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to lift your arm diagonally upward.
Inhale to slowly return the handle.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep your shoulders down and neck relaxed.
2.Keep your torso stable, avoiding rotation or sagging.

actionLibraryList.title: Side-Kneeling Arm Press
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Start with one arm bent at your side. Inhale to press the arm upward until fully extended.
2.Exhale and bend the arm to slowly lower it back to the starting position.
3.Keep your torso facing forward throughout the movement.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770694148745.jpg","context":"1.Keep your shoulders down.\n2.Maintain a neutral spine.\n3.Keep your pelvis stable."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to exert force.
Inhale to slowly return.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep your shoulders down; avoid shrugging.
2.Maintain your center of gravity without swaying.

actionLibraryList.title: Swan
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lie prone with your arms naturally extended, forehead slightly lowered, spine elongated, and legs straight in contact with the mat. Inhale as you stabilize the shoulder blades downward, gently press the arms into the mat, and sequentially extend the upper back, opening the chest forward and upward while keeping the head and neck aligned with the spine.
2.Exhale as you engage the core and control the spine rolling down segment by segment, returning to the prone starting position. Repeat the movement.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770687890084.jpg","context":"1.Keep the shoulders down and stable.\n2.Keep the legs stable and in contact with the mat."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Inhale to extend the upper back and open the chest.
Exhale to engage the core and control the spine as it rolls down.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid pressing down with your arms and neglecting back engagement.
2.Avoid shrugging the shoulders or excessively tilting the neck backward.

actionLibraryList.title: Swan Scissors
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Inhale as you gently press your arms down and sequentially extend the upper back, lifting into the top position of the Swan.
2.Maintain the lifted upper back, engage the hamstrings, and lift the legs off the mat, slightly abducted. Keep an even breath and engage the inner thigh muscles as you perform small, controlled inward scissor motions with the legs in the air.
3.Stop the scissor movement, exhale as you engage the core, and articulate the spine down segment by segment, returning to the prone starting position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770687967912.jpg","context":"1.Keep the shoulders down and stable."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Maintain even breathing during the scissor movement at the top.
Inhale to extend upward, exhale to stabilize and roll back down.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage the back, core, and legs.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep the back stable and avoid collapsing the lower spine.
2.Perform small, controlled scissor movements without swinging the legs.

actionLibraryList.title: Prone Triceps Extension
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Exhale and initiate the movement by engaging the back and rear delts, sweeping the straight arms back to the sides of the body.
2.Inhale, maintain tension through the back, keep the upper arms fixed, and bend the elbows to bring the rope back.
3.Exhale and extend the elbows, driving the movement with the triceps.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770628284214.jpg","context":"1.Keep your neck long and neutral, with the shoulders pulled down.\n2.Engage your core."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale on exertion.
Inhale on the return.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage the shoulders and upper back.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid arching the lower back.
2.Don’t overextend the neck.
3.Avoid shrugging your shoulders.

actionLibraryList.title: Prone Airplane
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Start with your arms extended to the sides. Exhale to bring your arms inward toward your body.
2.Inhale and slowly open your arms, controlling the handles as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770628239781.jpg","context":"1.Keep your neck in neutral extension and shoulders down.\n2.Engage your core."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to exert force.
Inhale to return.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid arching your lower back.
2.Keep your neck from overextending backward.
3.Keep your shoulders down; avoid shrugging.

actionLibraryList.title: Prone Arm Circles
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Start with your arms extended to the sides. Exhale to bring your arms inward toward your body.
2.Inhale and slowly draw your arms downward in an arc, controlling the handles as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770628261345.jpg","context":"1.Keep your neck in neutral extension and shoulders down.\n2.Engage your core."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to exert force.
Inhale to return.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid arching your lower back.
2.Keep your neck from overextending backward.
3.Keep your shoulders down; avoid shrugging.

actionLibraryList.title: Shoulder Stretch
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lightly engage the core, keep the pelvis neutral and stable, and let the shoulders relax down.
2.Inhale to prepare. Extend one arm straight across the chest, keeping the palm relaxed and the shoulder blade stable without shrugging. Use the opposite forearm or hand to gently press the arm closer to the chest, feeling a gentle stretch across the rear shoulder, posterior deltoid, and surrounding shoulder joint. Breathe evenly while holding the stretch.
3.Exhale slowly to release back to neutral, then repeat on the other side.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770691152685.jpg","context":"1.Keep the shoulders relaxed and down.\n2.Maintain a neutral spine and stable pelvis."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Breathe evenly while holding the stretch.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Feel a stretch around the shoulder joint.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep the shoulders relaxed and down.
2.Draw the ribs in, avoid flaring, and keep the torso aligned without twisting.

actionLibraryList.title: Chest Stretch
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lightly engage the core, keep the pelvis neutral and stable, lengthen the spine, and let the shoulders relax down.
2.Inhale to prepare. With both arms straight, stack them in front of the body to one side, keeping the elbows extended and the shoulder blades stable. Exhale as the rib cage leads the upper body into an open rotation to the opposite side, reaching the top arm back behind the body. Lift and open the sternum to feel a gentle stretch across the front of the chest and shoulders. Breathe evenly.
3.Exhale with control to return to neutral and repeat on the other side.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770691265549.jpg","context":"1.Keep your shoulders relaxed and down.\n2.Keep the pelvis stable."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Breathe evenly.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Feel a stretch across the front of the chest and shoulders.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid shrugging the shoulders.
2.Keep the pelvis neutral and level.

actionLibraryList.title: Mat Open Book
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lie on your side in a neutral spine position. Relax the chest, align the pelvis and legs in a straight line with heels together. Extend the top arm forward at chest height. Engage the core.
2.Inhale, rotate the thoracic spine backward while opening the top arm horizontally toward the floor, keeping the pelvis and lower body stable.
3.Exhale, rotate the thoracic spine back to the starting position, bringing the arm back to the initial placement.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770689393075.jpg","context":"1.Let the shoulders relax and sink naturally.\n2.Keep the pelvis vertical to the floor."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Inhale to rotate the thoracic spine and open the arm.
Exhale to return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep the pelvis stable; avoid tilting or rocking forward/backward.
2.Keep the shoulders relaxed; avoid shrugging.
3.Maintain control of the arm; avoid swinging or using momentum.
